title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot deduces that Patrick and Christine Redfern conspired to murder Elena Marshall. He concludes that Elena was hiding in a "fairy hole," evidenced by her perfume, while a decoy—likely Christine using brown sunscreen to alter her appearance—lay on the beach at Fairy Bay wearing a Chinese-style hat to mimic a corpse. Poirot identifies a flaw in the killers' plan: the loss of scissors used to cut an identical green hat. He determines that Patrick strangled Elena after 11:45, a time manipulated by the killers. Poirot breaks Christine's alibi by proving she had two opportunities to tamper with Linda Marshall's watch: once in Linda's room and again after Linda exited the water. He also dismisses Christine's claim of acrophobia as a lie. Comparing the case to the Alice Corey murder, Poirot suggests that Patrick's signature method involves "playing with time" and posits that he may be a serial killer.

In separate conversations, Poirot explains his reasoning to Mrs. Gardner and comforts Linda Marshall, who admits to feeling relief after destroying a wax figure of her stepmother. Meanwhile, Kenneth Marshall confronts Rosamond Darnley, revealing he suspected her of murder because of her past violent behavior and her attempt to provide him with a false alibi. Kenneth expresses disdain for Elena, describing her as a pitiful, helpless woman. The tension between Kenneth and Rosamond shifts toward their relationship; Kenneth demands that Rosamond abandon her profitable clothing business to move to the countryside with him, threatening that he will not be with her otherwise. Rosamond sarcastically accepts, noting that her dream of living in the countryside is coming true.
